incorrects.description: Student set incorrect heights on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the data table. What number is next to Salad? What number is next to Pasta?

incorrects.description: Student set incorrect heights on second attempt

params.description: Axis numbers highlight to show the scale increments.

steps.text: The axis counts by 5s. Salad has 15. Count up: 5, 10, 15. Where does 15 land? Pasta has 25. Count: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. Set each bar to end at its number.

incorrects.description: Student set incorrect heights three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates: Salad bar rises to 15 on the axis, then Pasta bar rises to 25.

steps.text: Let me show you. Salad has 15. I find 15 on the axis and set the bar to end there. Pasta has 25. I find 25 on the axis and set that bar to end there. The bar height matches the value on the axis. That is how bar graphs work: the height shows the count.
